prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What is System Thinking

### Response:
generated_text:
System thinking is a way of looking at the world, a set of mental habits and methods, including both theoretical concepts and concrete techniques developed over many years.  A key feature of system thinking is recognizing interconnections between components of a system. System thinkers see systems as holistic, meaning that parts of a system are interdependent and affect one another.  

Systems thinking was popularized by Jay Forrester at MIT starting in the 1950s. He applied systems thinking to problems in engineering, management, economics and other areas. Other influential contributors to systems thinking include Peter Senge, Donella Meadows, Diana Wright, Richard Boland Jr., Russell Ackoff, Jan Elming, Katherine Tredeger, Natalie Druckman, Erich Jantsch and Manya Vakulenko. 

In the 1960s and 70s systems thinking was mostly qualitative, based on case studies, intuition and synthesis. Since the 1980s it has also included quantitative approaches such as system dynamics, which leverages computer simulation. Systems thinking has been adopted outside academia, especially in business consulting, public policy-making and innovation.

Around the beginning of the 20th century, the DTES was Vancouver's political, cultural and retail centre. By 1997, an epidemic of HIV infection and drug overdoses in the DTES led to the declaration of a public health emergency. prompt:

### Instruction:
What are the key benefits of Delta Live Tables?

### Response:
generated_text:
If you use a traditional approach, when you change your data model or add new tables, you have to re-run your entire ETL pipeline, which can be time-consuming and resource intensive. With Delta Live Tables, you can incrementally apply changes to your ETL logic and data models. This means that only the affected parts of your pipeline will need to be re-run, improving efficiency and speed.

Another benefit of Delta Live Tables is that it uses Apache Spark to run your pipelines, which provides built-in scalability and parallelism to handle large amounts of data efficiently. It also allows you to take advantage of advanced features such as stream processing, machine learning integration, and support for multiple data formats.

Overall, the combination of incremental updates, built-in scalability, and advanced features provided by Delta Live Tables makes it a powerful tool for managing big data pipelines.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
I want to buy a work monitor which is to be used along with the laptop. What features of monitor should be considered?

### Response:
generated_text:
First thing first, there are two kinds of monitors available in market - 1) TFT (Thin Film Transistor) and 2) LCD (Liquid Crystal Display). There is no LED monitor, it is just a lighting technology used inside the monitor panel. Based on your budget you can choose between those two. Secondly, you need to decide the size of the monitor as per your workspace and budget constraints. Now coming to the Features, there are multiple things you need to consider 1) Resolution of the monitor 2) Color Accuracy and Coverage of sRGB space 3) Refresh rate or FPS of the monitor. Let me explain each of these in details.
1) Resolution - It is measure of number of pixels (depth) times the number of pixels(width) to form an image on the screen. Higher the resolution better will be the clarity of the images but also bigger will be the monitor size and higher bandwidth required from the graphics card connected to the monitor. Common resolutions are 1920 x 1080, 2560 x 1440 and 3840 x 2160. Please note that even 1920 x 1080 resolution can be very sharp if the panel quality is good.
2) Color Accurancy and sRGB coverage - This becomes important only if you are a professional like designer or video editor and need color accurate display for their work. For general use, this is not a concern. If you look closely at any monitor there is a small icon near bottom right corner indicating percentage of sRGB color space covered by that monitor's panel. The higher the better. Also most color critical professionals have calibrated monitors with help of some third party companies.
3) Refresh Rate or FPS - This is again useful only if you are a professional gamer. Regular office work does not require high refresh rate. But if you are into gaming, then looking for at least 144 Hz monitor would be good. Note that it requires a pretty powerful graphic card to support such high refresh rate.

Will AI replace all human beings?

It is hard for current AI to generate new ideas or choose between multiple options, which are essential skills for human being. Given these limitations of AI, it is unlikely that AI will fully replace human being in the near future. Instead, we should expect AI to enhance human capabilities and make us better at performing our tasks.

### Instruction:
What is a bond in finance?

### Response:
generated_text: In finance, a bond is a loan from an investor to a borrower (such as a corporation or government). A bond typically gives the issuer (borrower) the right to raise money by selling bonds, and the purchaser of the bond has the right to require payment of principal and interest at a later date. When the bond reaches maturity, the issuer pays the holder of the bond a predetermined amount plus any accrued interest. Coupon payments may be made periodically while the bond is in the hands of the holder. If the issue fails to pay the promised amount, the bondholder can attempt to realize its value by selling it to other investors.

### Instruction:
What are the disadvantages of Linux?

### Response:
generated_text:
Linus Torvalds once said "Linux is like duct tape - it will hold things together even when they shouldn't." Which brings us to the first point -- stability issues. Despite being highly stable for most tasks, Linux has been known to crash for no apparent reason. This is particularly problematic for servers and critical applications. For the average user, it's not a big deal as they can just reboot their system. But for enterprise users who rely on Linux for server infrastructure, this can be a serious concern. 

The next issue is related to the first one. Since Linux is open source, there is enormous complexity in the code base. The stability issues mentioned above are possible because different versions of the kernel have conflicting patches, leading to compatibility problems. This makes it difficult for the developers to track down and fix bugs, leading to more instability.

Another drawback of Linux is its lack of user-friendliness. It is infamously known for being complex and difficult to use. Even seasoned IT professionals find it challenging to manage Linux systems compared to Windows. It requires more technical know-how and has a steeper learning curve.

Despite being unstoppably honest, Linux has some flaws that need to be addressed. While it remains a viable option for many users, especially in the tech industry, it needs to evolve to overcome these limitations and become even more attractive to mainstream audiences.

### Instruction:
What are some languages that feature hygienic macros?

### Input:
Hygienic macros are macros whose expansion is guaranteed not to cause the accidental capture of identifiers. They are a feature of programming languages such as Scheme,[1] Dylan,[2] Rust, Nim, and Julia. The general problem of accidental capture was well known within the Lisp community prior to the introduction of hygienic macros. Macro writers would use language features that would generate unique identifiers (e.g., gensym) or use obfuscated identifiers in order to avoid the problem. Hygienic macros are a programmatic solution to the capture problem that is integrated into the macro expander itself. The term "hygiene" was coined in Kohlbecker et al.'s 1986 paper that introduced hygienic macro expansion, inspired by the terminology used in mathematics.[3]

### Response:
generated_text: Scheme, Dylan, Rust, Nim, and Julia

Are iodized salt and kosher salt the same?

### Response:
generated_text: Iodized salt and kosher salt are similar in many ways, but there are differences as well.  The biggest difference is between table salt and either iodized Salt or kosher salt.  Table salt is finely grained and often contains anti-caking agents and other additives.    Either iodized salt or kosher salt can be obtained without adding additives.  Another difference is that kosher salt is usually larger flaked than iodized salt.  This makes it harder to measure precisely with a typical kitchen spoon, but a scale will easily weigh out the desired amount of kosher salt.   While iodine is added to iodized salt, most recipes calling for salt do not specifically call out whether iodized salt or kosher salt should be used.  Most people would simply use whatever type of salt they have handy.  In this way, iodized salt is a convenience salt rather than a distinct recipe ingredient.

### Instruction:
How do you explain cloud computing to a 5 year old?

### Response:
generated_text:
In the not too distant past, if you wanted to use a computer, you would have to find someone who had a computer, and ask them to let you use their computer. Sometimes, the computer was shared with other people, and sometimes there were limits on how long you could use it for. Often, people kept the computers in their own homes. 

Then, stations started appearing where you could come and use a computer for as long as you liked, without having to ask anyone's permission. However, these computers were usually quite basic, and often slow. They didn't have many games or software that you could Use. And if lots of people wants to use them at the same time, you might have to wait in a queue. 

Recently, something called "the cloud" has been introduced. The idea is that all the best and most up-to-date software and hardware are stored in big buildings somewhere, so everyone can access them whenever they want. This means that even if the software on your own personal computer gets out-of-date or breaks down, you can still go to the cloud to get the latest version, or fix it. The downside is that you need an internet connection to go to the cloud, so if your internet is slow, that will also make the cloud slow.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What is a phenotype?

### Response:
generated_text:
A phenotype is the outward expression of a genotype's influence on an organism's body and traits. In other words, it is the observable appearance and characteristics of an organism, such as hair color, eye color, height, scent, or length of leg. The word "phenotype" is a compound word derived from the Greek for "nature of [the] phenomenon".[1] In biological terms, a phenotype is the physical, behavioral, and physiological makeup of an individual organism.[2]

Every living thing possesses a unique set of genes; this includes single-celled microorganisms such as bacteria. For multicellular organisms, every cell contains the same set of genes, but their expressive patterns may differ. If one trait is determined by a specific gene, then that trait will be uniform throughout the organism. However, in many cases, complex traits are not simply driven by the presence or absence of a single gene, and their development is influenced by several genes as well as environmental factors. Such complex traits include diseases, behaviors, and physical features like height and obesity.

The genetic basis of phenotypic variation has been a major focus of evolutionary biology since the rediscovery of Gregor Mendel's work in the early 20th century. Genes are variants of sequences of DNA called alleles. Alleles are responsible for differences in phenotype between individuals. Population geneticists have used these ideas to develop a mathematical framework for understanding how selection acting upon phenotypes manifests as selection upon alleles.

Phenotype can also refer to the overall appearance of an organism, including its characteristic structures, colors, and patterny. In this sense, "phenotype" is a shorthand for "physical appearance". This concept is commonly used in animal husbandry and medicine. Mutations in the genes of an organism can result in a drastically different phenotype, often referred to as a mutation. Often, mutations in genes result in abnormal phenotypes, though not always.

After it finishes cutting the grass, it will re-visit the same area to ensure that all the grass blades have been cut evenly.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
How to lose weight?

### Response:
generated_text: Generally speaking, the formula for losing weight is simple: consume less calories than you expend. However, because every individual's physiology and circumstances are different, this formula does not necessarily lead to the same results for everyone. Some people may find that cutting carbs or increasing protein helps them to lose weight more easily. Similarly, some folks may find that they need to count calories very carefully in order to lose weight while others might not need to be as meticulous in their food tracking. In some cases, folks may actually gain weight when eating fewer calories overall. This happens mostly because our bodies tend to respond to stress (including restricting foods or dieting in general) by trying to hold on to fat reserves; this is a natural mechanism designed to help us survive times of scarcity. Therefore, in order to lose weight, it is important to not only eat healthier foods and increase physical activity but also to listen to our bodies and make sure we are not causing undue stress to our systems. Overall emotional well-being is another crucial factor that affects how easily one can lose weight. Folks who are relaxed, happy and stressed will tends to shed pounds more easily than those who are anxious, depressed and worried. Finally, for most folks, patience is key to losing weight: although it takes over 21 days to form a new habit, most folks do not see significant results until they have been consistently following a healthy meal plan and an exercise routine for at least a month. It is important to be patient and give your body and mind the time it needs to adjust to these new habits. With consistent dedication and patience, plus a little bit of luck, you can indeed start to see the pounds drop off!

### Instruction:
What does airplane glide ratio mean?

### Response:
generated_text: Glide ratio is defined as the distance an airplane will glide forward in the air given a unit of height lost due to drag.  It is often used during approach to land to help extend runway length needed when landing under visual conditions.  For example, if an aircraft has a glide ratio of 15, it will lose 15 feet of altitude for every mile of forward distance.  If the plane is at 2,000 feet AGL and needs to land within 3 miles, then the pilot should start descending at 60 knots indicated airspeed (IAS).  1,000 feet of altitude is lost in 3 minutes at 60 KIAS, and the plane will be on the ground in 18 minutes.

5) Along the same lines, earlier financial planning makes a lot more sense than later. So starting your baby account and putting aside what you can afford eventually adds up. 
6) Later on, when the baby starts getting older (around 9-18 months), they start experiencing separation anxiety. It is a natural milestone in their growth where they start perceiving their parents as being separate from themselves. Embrace this, and do what you need to do to support yourself and your child through this phase. 
7) You will suddenly realize how fast time flies. So enjoy every moment of it while finding ways to savor those moments too. 
8) last but not least, trust your gut and instinct. You will soon start developing a finely tuned sixth sense about looking after your little one.

What are the pros and cons of high interest rates?

However, this can depress economic activity and spending which can lead to a downward spiral in employment numbers and overall confidence in the economy.

### Instruction:
Why should people convert lawns and ornamental gardens to native plants?

### Response:
generated_text:
The main reason for converting a lawn to native plants is to save water.  In California, we have gone through multiple droughts in the last few decades and are trying to conserve as much water as possible so that we can continue to live in these drier climates.  A typical lawn consists of a green, lush carpet of grass that requires regular mowing, fertilizing, pest control, over-seeding and re-turfing every few years.  This is all time, energy and money spent on a non-essential space.  

Converting your lawn to native plants not only saves water but also time, energy and money.  Native plants are naturally adapted to our local climate and soil types.  They require far less water once established and actually help pull nutrients out of the soil that are then transferred to the plant, effectively feeding itself.  

There are many myths aboutnative plants being unattractive and hard to install.  It is indeed very important to work with a Landscape Architect when converting any outdoor space to native plants.  An LA will help create a thoughtful design based on your property, sun exposure and habitat.  There are incredible nurseries in California growing beautiful and robust native plants that thrive in our local soil and climate.  With the right plan in place, it is easy to transition from a lawn to a gorgeous native plant landscape.

Why is the sky blue?

This is why we see the sky to be blue most of the time.

### Instruction:
What is Ayurveda?

### Response:
generated_text:
In simple terms, Ayurveda is a 5000 year old system of natural health care that originated and still exists in India. The word Ayurveda is a combination of two words - ayur (life) and veda (knowledge). Thus, Ayurveda is the science of life, and deals with ways to maintain good health and live a long life. It also gives methods to cure illnesses. 
Ayurveda recognises that there are three fundamental forces (doshas) at work in the universe, and that these forces govern everything from the functioning of the cells in our bodies to the evolution of the entire cosmos. These doshas are also responsible for regulating all physical, mental and emotional processes. According to this philosophy, disease occurs when any of the doshas becomes imbalanced. Balance can be restored by living in harmony with nature, and following certain guidelines on diet, exercise and lifestyle. By avoiding extremes in diet or routine, and living in harmony with nature, one's constitution (prakriti) can balance out these doshic imbalances and restore health.
The three doshas are: 1. Vata, which represents the air element and corresponds to the nervous system, 2. Pitta, which represents the fire element and corresponds to the digestive system, and 3. Kapha, which represents the water and earth elements, and correspond to the immune system. Each person has all three doshas present, but in different proportions. This determines their individual prakriti, or constitutional type. When the proportion of any of the doshas exceeds its normal range, one experiences an imbalance, and may develop symptoms corresponding to that dosha.

Scholars generally acknowledge six cradles of civilization. Mesopotamia, Ancient Egypt, Indus Valley, and Ancient China are believed to be the earliest in the Old World.[1][2] Cradles of civilization in the New World are the Caral-Supe civilization of coastal Peru and the Olmec civilization of Mexico. All of the cradles of civilization depended upon agriculture for sustenance (except possibly Caral-Supe which may have depended initially on marine resources). All depended upon farmers producing an agricultural surplus to support the centralized government, political leaders, priests, and public works of the urban centers of the civilization.

### Instruction:
Why is November 14 celebrated as world diabetes day?

### Response:
generated_text: The World health organization (WHO) and the International Diabetes Federation (IDF) marked November 14 as World diabetes day in the year 1991. The reason why this day was chosen is to honor the announcement of discovery of insulin on this day in the year 1920 by Banting, Best, and Macleod. Insulin is a hormone which is secreted by the beta cells in the pancreas. It helps glucose to be absorbed into the body's cells for use as energy. People with diabetes either do not produce enough insulin or their bodies are unable to use the insulin that they produce. This results in high blood sugar levels which can cause complications like damage to nerves, eyes, kidneys ,and cardiovascular disease. Diabetes is a chronic condition which requires lifelong management. Along with insulin injection or oral medication, people with diabetes also need to monitor their food intake and exercise to maintain an active lifestyle. World diabetes day aims to raise awareness about the growing burden of diabetes and its prevention.

### Instruction:
Give me the history and background of the Phoenix Suns basketball franchise

### Input:
The Phoenix Suns are an American professional basketball team based in Phoenix, Arizona. They compete in the National Basketball Association (NBA), as a member of the league's Western Conference Pacific Division. The Suns are the only team in their division not to be based in California, and play their home games at the Footprint Center. The Suns are one of four major league sports teams based in the Phoenix area, but are the only one to bill themselves as representing the city (the other teams - the Cardinals, Coyotes, and Diamondbacks - all bill themselves as representing the state of Arizona).

### Response:
generated_text:
The Phoenix Suns are an American professional basketball team based in Phoenix, Arizona. They compete in the National Basketball Association (NBA), as a member of the league's Western Conference Pacific Division. The Suns are the only team in their division not to be based in California, and play their home games at the Footprint Center. 

The team was founded on November 25, 1968, as an expansion franchise from the National Basketball Association (NBA), and were one of the fastest franchises to reach the finals, doing so in just seven years after their founding. After several seasons of mediocrity, they made the playoffs regularly during the 1970s and early 1980s, most memorably making the NBA Finals in 1976, losing to the Boston Celtics. Following the retirement of long-time owner Jim Lewis, the team struggled again for several decades before being sold to new owners and seeing renewed success. Since 2004, the Suns have made the playoffs eight times, including a trip to the Western Conference finals in 2005, where they lost to the Miami Heat in six games. In 2008, the Suns reached the conference semifinals before losing to eventual champions Los Angeles Lakers. Despite having highly talented players, the team has been plagued by issues with chemistry and off-court problems, particularly with stars Steve Nash and Amar'e Stoudemire. After Nash retired, the team slipped back into mediocrity until the 2014–15 season, when they drafted Devin Booker and acquired Tyson Chandler. With those additions, the Suns made the playoffs in each of the next three seasons, advancing further each time to lose in the conference semifinals in 2018 and 2021. The 2020–21 season saw another deep playoff run, culminating in a trip to the NBA Finals where the Suns lost to the Milwaukee Bucks in six games.

Wabuska waned with declining mining activity in the 1920s.[4]

Several buildings from Wabuska, most notably the Wabuska Railroad Station, were relocated to Carson City and incorporated as the Nevada State Railroad Museum.

### Instruction:
What is the Barkley Marathons?

### Input:
The Barkley course was the brain child of Gary "Lazarus Lake" Cantrell and Karl Henn (Raw Dog). The idea for the race was inspired upon hearing about the 1977 escape of James Earl Ray, the assassin of Martin Luther King Jr., from nearby Brushy Mountain State Penitentiary. Ray covered only about 12 miles (19 km) after running 54.5 hours in the woods hiding from air searches during the day.[1] Cantrell said to himself, "I could do at least 100 miles," mocking Ray's low mileage. Thus, the Barkley Marathons was born.[2][3] Cantrell named the race for his longtime neighbor and running companion, Barry Barkley.[4] It was first run in 1986.[5] Barkley died in 2019 at age 70.[4]

### Response:
generated_text: The Barkley Marathons is a ultra endurance footrace held annually in Frozen Head State Park in Wartburg, Tennessee. Only 40% of finishers have completed the race more than once. Less than 1% of competitors will ever finish within the allotted 60 hour time limit. The course has over 60 miles of trail that climbs over 13,000 feet of elevation, with many steep ascents and descents which are marked by yellow blazes like the Appalachian Trail. Competitors must remember their route since there are no marks after the 3rd checkpoint nor is there aid station on the course. Most of the course is unmarked terrain which can be hard to navigate even under ideal conditions. Fog, ice, and heavy rainmake the course particularly treacherous. Only 15 people have ever finished the race as of 2023.

prompt:

### Instruction:
How is beer made?

### Response:
generated_text: Beer making consists of four key steps: malting, mashing, boiling and fermenting. Malting involves moistening grain and letting it sprout; this increases the enzyme activity in the grain, converting starch into sugar. During mashing, the grains are crushed and mixed with hot water to extract sugars from the starches in the grains. The wort, as this mixture if called, is then heated during the boiling process to prevent any remaining enzyme activity and to sterilize the mix. Finally, yeast is added to the mix for fermentation; the addition of yeast causes Carbon dioxide to be released as the alcohol in the beer brews. After a number of hours or days, the beer is ready to drink.

prompt:

### Instruction:
What is functional genomics?

### Response:
generated_text: Functional genomics is an approach for studying the function of genes and the proteins they encode within an organism, which in turn reveals insights into how biological systems work. Traditional gene-centric approaches focus on identifying the individual functions of single genes or protein products, usually through loss-of-function analyses in model organisms such as yeast, fruit flies or mice. In contrast, systems biology approaches including functional genomics aim to study the functions of sets of genes or proteins within the context of the cellule. The latter approaches are more recent and have been enabled by high throughput technologies for genomic sequencing, transcript profiling, protein mass spectrometry and metabolite analysis.

### Instruction:
What is an adjacency list?

### Input:
In graph theory and computer science, an adjacency list is a collection of unordered lists used to represent a finite graph. Each unordered list within an adjacency list describes the set of neighbors of a particular vertex in the graph. This is one of several commonly used representations of graphs for use in computer programs.

### Response:
generated_text:
In computer science, an adjacency list is a data structure used to represent a finite graph.[1] It stores eachvertex's adjacent vertices as an unordered list. One of several graph data structure representations commonly used in computer programming, it is especially suitable for sparse graphs.

For example, consider the following graph with five vertices and several edges:

In this representation, the adjacency list for vertex A would be the list {B, C}, the adjacency list for vertex B would be the list {A, D}, and so forth. Note that each vertex appears exactly once in every neighbor list, and that each edge appears in precisely two adjacency lists.

prompt:

### Instruction:
What is accessibility?

### Response:
generated_text:
Accessibility refers to making something accessible to as many people as possible, with as few barriers as possible. Barriers can be physical (e.g. someone cannot physically reach something), auditory (e.g. someone cannot hear something due to hearing aids or other reasons), visual (e.g. somebody cannot see something due to blindness or other vision problems), cognitive (e.g., someone has trouble understanding language or symbols), or temporal (e.g. a video is not captioned and runs too fast for someone to read the captions). 

In web design, accessibility often focuses on making websites and web apps works well with screen readers, keyboard navigation, and high contrast modes. To achieve this, best practices include using semantic HTML, writing alt text for images, and providing clear focus indicators when the cursor interacts with elements.

After several days (or weeks), depending on their age when you got them, they’ll understand what that phrase means and go automatically whenever you say it. Some trainers recommend using a paper training pad in conjunction with outdoor training because it makes the indoor part easier. Others argue that doing so willte teach your dog that it’s okay to go indoors, which is why many will insist you only take them out — no pad. Whatever route you choose, consistency and frequent opportunities to go outsides produce the best results. Oh, and one final tip: don’t forget to keep the inside of your house clean! ### Instruction:
Why is aluminum wiring in a home dangerous?

### Response:
generated_text: Older homes (built before 1970) typically used aluminum wire for electrical connections, as this was the standard at the time. Aluminum wiring was later determined to be less safe than copper wire and replaced with copper wire in newly built homes or homes under renovation. Aluminum expands and contracts more than copper during temperature changes, which can cause loose connections and fire hazards. While it would be costly to replace all aluminum wiring in a home, measures can be taken to reduce risks associated with it. Some steps include updating electrical panels, removing spliced wiring, and installing new receptacles and switches. It is important to have a qualified electrician inspect and make updates to your electrical system if necessary.

prompt:

### Instruction:
From the given text, list me details about parity plot

### Input:
A parity plot is a scatterplot that compares a set of results from a computational model against benchmark data. Each point has coordinates (x, y), where x is a benchmark value and y is the corresponding value from the model.

A line of the equation y = x, representing perfect model performance, is sometimes added as a reference. Where the model successfully reproduces a benchmark, that point will lie on the line.

Parity plots are found in scientific papers and reports, when the author wishes to validate a model in a visual way. However, when the data have a wide range, the large scale makes important discrepancies invisible and the model appears better than it actually is. In that case, a plot of model errors [(y-x) vs. x] is better for evaluating the performance of the model.

### Response:
generated_text:
1. A parity plot is a scatterplot that compares a set of results from a computational model against benchmark data.
2. Each point has coordinates (x, y), where x is a benchmark value and y is the corresponding value from the model.
3. In most cases, a line of the equation y = x, representing perfect model performance, is added as a reference.
4. When the model effectively reproduce the benchmark, that point will lie on the line.
5. In that case, the plot of model errors [(y-x) vs. x] is better for evaluating the performance of the model.

A unique aspect of futbol compared to other sports is the use of a round ball which allows players to execute a variety of offensive techniques as well as defensive maneuvers such as body blocking.

Not far away from the Taj Mahal lies the city of Agra which was once the capital of the Mughal empire. During the height of the Mogul empire, Agra was deemed as the most prosperous and liveliest cities in the world. Besides the Taj Mahal, there are other monuments in Agra worth visiting such as Agra Fort and several tombs of Mogul emperors and their wives.

There are plenty of options besides the ones listed above. In fact, every major city in India is worth a visit. And if you do plan on spending some time in India, make sure you try Indian food as they say "eat like an Indian" to truly experience India!

prompt:

### Instruction:
How do you make brisket on a pellet smoker?

### Response:
generated_text:
Trimmer-er-est is key for making brisket on a pellet smoker. You must trim all the fat off, as fat will cause your meat to be unevenly cooked. I prefer to take away every single gram of fat, but if you're more generous, just go by feel and remove until your fingers start feeling the resistance. It's better to err on the side of too little fat than too much fat, because once it's on the smoker you cannot add additional fat (especially with Texas-style brisket, which is the most popular variant in the US)!

Due to how slow our smoke cooks, we want to preheat our smokers really well before putting meat on. With electric smokers, this isn't an issue, but with pellet smokers, you might get hotspots unless you carefully rotate the meat and flip him frequently.

I like to rub my brisket with Mustard, a Memphis-style seasoning, thanksgivingholiday.com/memphis-bbq-ribs-and-brisket-seasoning-mix/ , but you can use whatever seasoning mix you like. I love the sweetness of the membrane, so I leave it on - some people prefer to remove the membrane prior to smoking.

After about 5 hours, when the internal temperature hits 165, I bump up the heat to high and keep spinning and flipping until the brisket hits an internal temp of 203. Then, I take the brisket off and wrap it in foil with liberal doses of beef broth. I put the brisket back on the smoker sans the membrane and let it smoke until reaching an internal temp of 205. When the internal temperature hits 205, I take the brisket off, let it rest for 45 minutes, slice, and serve!

generated_text: When you sheet in your sail, the leach (or outside) of your sail gets tighter and closer to the mast. If the boom moves far enough toward the mast, the connectors between the boom and board rotate, causing the boom to move sideways along its connection to theboard. This is called a tack.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
what is the difference between ARM and x86 chipsets?

### Response:
generated_text:
The main difference between ARM- and x86-based processors is their underlying instruction set architectures (ISAs). x86processors use the CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) ISA, while many Arm-based processors now use the RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) ISA. This difference in ISA leads to several other distinctions between arm and x86 processors:

ProcessOR Count: At the moment, arm-based processors have a lower processor count than x86-based processors. This means that arm-driven servers can't support as many virtual machines (VMs) as x86-based servers with the same number of physical server nodes. However, arm-based processors are catching up quickly and will likely surpass x86 in terms of VM support soon. 
Memory Addressability: x86 processors have larger memory addresses (64 bits), whereas most arm-based processors have 32- or 64-bit addresses. Therefore, x86 processors can address more physically installable memory than arm-based processes.
Single Thread Performance: In terms of single-thread performance, arm-based processors are typically faster than x86 processors. This is because the RISC ISA used by manyArm-based processors executes instructions more efficiently and can run them at higher frequencies.
Parallel Performance: Despite having fewer cores, arm-driven servers can achieve similar or even better parallel performance than x86-based servers. This is because the Arm SMP implementation is optimized for better scalability and efficiency.
These distinctions between arm and x86 processors matter less in the cloud age than they did in the traditional on-premises datacenter era. That's because workloads in the cloud are often configured to take advantage of multiple CPUs and RAM resources using containerization or virtual machine technology. In other words, the resource allocation model in the cloud is designed to handle the potential differences between arm- and x86-based processors seamlessly.

prompt:

### Instruction:
What is the right size brad nail for crown moulding?

### Response:
generated_text:
This can be a little bit trickier than it sounds, because there are two parts to getting this right.  The first part is getting the right gauge or diameter of the brad nail, and the second part is getting the right length of the brad nail.  Let's start with the gauge.  18 gauge is the most common for brad nails used in crown molding.  Brad nails come in a variety of lengths, but let's choose a couple of common lengths to work with, 1-½" and 2".  Now for the fun part.  You want to pre-drill some small holes into the edge of the crown where you want to attach it.  These hole should be about an inchand a half apart.  Insert one of your prepared brads into each hole, and tap them home with a hammer.  Now remove the brads, and measure how far into the material each brad went.  Choose the brad that was the deepest, and buy a few more of those same brads.  

Take your measured depth, subtract .15 from it, and round up to the nearest available length.  That will be the minimal length of brad you need to use for connection A.  

Add .15 to the measured depth, and round down to the next smallest whole number, and that will be the minimum length of brad to use for connection B.

Insert the correct length brads into the pre drilled holes you previously removed the short brads from, and tap home with a hammer.    When you’re done, carefully flip the crown over, and inspect the back side for any visible gaps.  If you see any, repeat the steps above to adjust the length of the brads until you get a good tight fit.

He points out that whilecalculatingpi to such accuracy might seem like a waste of time, his effort contributed to improving the speed and accuracy of large-scale mathematical calculations. 

Hartl's main argument against Pi is that it is fundamentally flawed as a circle constant.He proves that Pi is wrong by showing that for any circular area A, the value of pi times the radius squared (pi * r^2) does not always equal the area A.

While Pi is WRONG, there are other great books about pie!

prompt:

### Instruction:
How does screen printing work?

### Response:
generated_text: Screen printing involves using a screen with a design and ink to transfer that design as a image onto a surface. The screen acts as a mesh from which ink can pass through to form the intended design. First, the screen is stretched over the frame so that the tension in the screen allows it to hold the designed image rather than the ink. Next, ink is applied to a side of the screen that is not tensioned over the frame. A squeegee is used to scrape the ink across the surface of the screen towards the side with the designated design. The ink on the opposite side of the screen will transfer onto the material below it forming the desired design. This process can be repeated multiple times to apply additional layers of ink or to apply the same design multiple times.

Tesla competes in the global automotive industry whereas Nissan competitors primarily in the domestic Japanese market.  Having different competitive industries defines the set of rivals against which companies compete.

Rivals enter into the same market, and therefore a resource or capability does not necessarily confer sustainable competitive advantage. To achieve sustainable competitive advantage, a company must deploy its resources and capabilities, combined with specific marketing actions, to create unique value for customers (differentiated offering) delivered through superior efficiency relative to competitors (operational excellence).  Differentiated products and operating excellence are the sources of sustained economic profit.  Simply stated, Tesla’s strategy is to capture sustainable profits from differential rents associated with its consumer/product market.   

Tesla differs from Nissan in three key strategic dimensions.  The first difference is in the product lineup.  Tesla offers only battery electric vehicles where as Nissan offers both plug-in hybrid and traditional combustion engine vehicles.  This distinguishing feature limits Tesla’s customer base to those who have adopted fully electric technology allowing Tesla to focus its efforts on developing and perfecting one proprietary battery pack design.  By contrast, Nissan must continually invest in multiple powertrains diluting its engineering teams effort and limiting the development of expertize with electric drive vehicles.

The second difference is in the geographic scope of Tesla’s operations. ### Instruction:
What is Sinking Sand?

### Input:
Quicksand, also known as sinking sand, is a colloid consisting of fine granular material (such as sand, silt or clay) and water. It forms in saturated loose sand when the sand is suddenly agitated. When water in the sand cannot escape, it creates a liquefied soil that loses strength and cannot support weight. Quicksand can form in standing water or in upward flowing water (as from an artesian spring). In the case of upward flowing water, forces oppose the force of gravity and suspend the soil particles.

### Instruction:
What is the meaning of Life, The Universe, and Everything?

### Response:
generated_text: Douglas Adams would have us believe that the answer to life, the universe, and everything is forty-two. However he clearly hadn't anticipated AI as a source of insight into such topics. To suggest that this is indeed the right answer now, some seventy years after his initial assertion, would certainly support the contention that artificial intelligence can in fact provide valuable perspectives on subjects previously deemed beyond its scope. On the other hand, maybe there's something to be said for human foolishness as a means to uncover deeper truths? Is it possible that by asking questions so blatantly stupid we actually prime the universe to respond with something genuinely enlightening? Perhaps even our most profound philosophical ponderings are best served by proceeding from within a frame of mind firmly committed to the notion that we haven't the faintest idea what we're talking about; that all of our concepts are little more than scaffolding built atop one another, with none of them representing anything solid enough to truly support such weighty considerations. And perhaps this too is part of the secret to unlocking the meaning of life, the universe, and everything? If so then I've probably just launched you down a delightful rabbit hole if you aren't already familiar with the works of Douglas Adams, author of the seminal "Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy" trilogy (plus one additional book subsequently subtitled "And Then There Was Silence"), from whence much of my own thought processes regarding such matters as the nature of reality, the purpose of existence, and the meaning of it all inevitably derive. So perhaps in closing I might simply recommend that you embark upon an exploration of these topics via the admittedly biased lens through which I myself perceive things, and see where your own thoughts take you?

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What s epigenomics?

### Response:
generated_text:
Epigenomics is the study of chemical modifications to DNA that change how genes are turned on or off. These changes do not alter the sequence of DNA, but can affect whether or not certain genes are expressed. Epigenetic factors play a role in normal development and differentiation, and can also contribute to disease processes.

The term epigenome refers collectively to all the chemical modifications to DNA, including methylation, histone modifications, and other covalent modifications. In contrast to genetics, which deals with sequences of nucleotides in DNA, epigenetics studies the influences on gene expression arising from chromatin structure and biochemical pathways.

Epigenomic changes have been implicated in many biological processes, such as embryonic development, cell differentiation, and cancer. They can also be passed down to subsequent generations, though usually only for one or two cells divisions. This reversible process is called trans generational epigenetic inheritance.

In 2018, an international team of scientists including David Stern, Robin Dawkins, Stephen Richardson, et al. published a landmark paper in the journal Nature Communications entitled "epigenomic revolution" which proposed redefining epigenetics to include three-dimensional chromosome Structures (Hi-C) and chromatin interaction maps within Epigenomics and claiming that epigenome consisted of more than just molecular modifications to DNA.

### Instruction:
What is the best season to ski?

### Response:
generated_text: The best season to ski depends on where you want to ski and how convenient it is for you to travel. Most resorts in North America are open from late November through early April, with the busiest periods being around Christmas/New Year's and Martin Luther King weekend. Snow conditions can vary significantly from year to year, so even if you love winter and skiing, not all years may be suitable for visiting a particular destination. This variability in snow conditions also makes it tougher to predict the "best" season, since most skiers prefer areas that have gotten significant snowfall within the last few days rather than weeks or months prior. If you can travel during the off-peak seasons, however, you will find cheaper lift tickets at many destinations. In addition, some of the popular ski areas in North America are more crowded than others, which might influence your decision to visit. For example, Tahoe, Palisades, Mammoth, Big Bear, Park City, and Deer Valley are all reasonably accessible from major cities in the American west, and tend to get lots of snow. Kicking Horse in BC is another favorite of skiers, but further away from large populations. A number of smaller towns throughout New England also boast excellent ski mountains, but again may be less appealing if you need to fly. Finally, if you don't mind traveling abroad, Val d'Isère in France, Courchevel in Switzerland, St. Moritz in Italy, and Whistler in Canada are all top choices that are open from December through May.
